title: Query One Column
summary: Learn how to retrieve a single column from a table using the SELECT statement in SQL.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to SELECT
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
The SELECT statement is used to retrieve data from a database. To query a single column, you specify the column name after the SELECT keyword. For example, to retrieve the 'name' column from the 'products' table, you would write:

```
SELECT name FROM products;
```

This query will return all the values in the 'name' column. Try it yourself by writing a query to select the 'category' column from the 'products' table.

sketchBlocks.title: Worked Example
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Let's say you want to find out all the product names in your catalog. You can use the following SQL query:

```
SELECT name FROM products;
```

This query will return a list of all product names stored in the 'products' table. Each row in the result corresponds to a product. Your turn: modify the query to select the 'price' column instead.

quizDraft.title: Fill in the Blank
quizDraft.prompt: Complete the SQL statement to select the 'category' column from the 'products' table: `SELECT [blank1] FROM products;`
quizDraft.hint: Think about which column you want to retrieve.

help.concept: The SELECT statement specifies which column to retrieve.
help.hint_1: Focus on the column name you need.
help.hint_2: The column is related to product types.

quizDraft.title: Understanding SELECT
quizDraft.prompt: What does the SELECT statement do in SQL?
options: It modifies data in a table.
It retrieves data from a table.
It deletes data from a table.
quizDraft.hint: Think about the purpose of SELECT in data retrieval.

help.concept: The SELECT statement is used to retrieve data from a database.
help.hint_1: SELECT is about fetching, not modifying.
